vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1696

plan.title: fix(render): project:render must emit registry items that pass vbrief:validate for mixed-status decompose groups

narratives.Description: task project:render groups scope vBRIEFs by GitHub issue number and assigns the grouped registry item one status (the umbrella's, e.g. cancelled) while still attaching the completed decomposed-story scopes as references. vbrief:validate's D3 registry-status rule then hard-fails because the item status (cancelled) contradicts the referenced scope plan.status (completed). A generator emitting artifacts its own validator rejects is a single-source-of-truth violation (#537). Make render output pass validate by construction.
narratives.Overview:
## What to build

Make `project:render` output pass `vbrief:validate` (D3 registry-status) for the canonical decompose pattern (cancelled umbrella + completed stories under one issue number).

Preferred structural fix: back the renderer's per-item status derivation and the validator's D3 check with ONE shared function, so render output cannot diverge from validate. Either emit one registry item per scope vBRIEF, or derive a grouped item's status with the same rule the validator enforces and never attach mixed-status scopes under a single contradicting status.

## Where

- packages/core/src/render/project-render.ts -- the grouping + status-assignment logic.
- packages/core/src/vbrief-validate/project-definition.ts -- the D3 registry-status rule (the validator side of the shared invariant).

## Out of scope

- Do NOT touch packages/core/src/render/roadmap-render.ts (owned by in-flight #1697) or any triage reader (owned by #1698).
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
- Extract a shared status-derivation/check function used by BOTH packages/core/src/render/project-render.ts (render side) and packages/core/src/vbrief-validate/project-definition.ts (D3 validate side).
- Either emit one registry item per scope vBRIEF, or compute the grouped item status via the shared function so it agrees with every attached scope's plan.status.
- Add a round-trip test: render -> validate passes on a fixture with a cancelled umbrella + multiple completed decomposed stories sharing one issue reference.
narratives.UserStory: As a deft operator reconciling lifecycle state, I want project:render output to pass vbrief:validate even when an issue was decomposed (cancelled umbrella + completed stories), so that I can commit the canonical re-render without the framework's own validator rejecting it.

items.title: render output passes validate for decompose groups

narrative.Acceptance: When project:render runs on a cancelled umbrella plus completed decomposed stories under one issue, the output passes vbrief:validate with zero D3 registry-status errors.
When the renderer assigns a registry item status, it uses the same shared function the D3 validator enforces, so render and validate cannot diverge.
When the round-trip test runs the mixed-status decompose fixture through render then validate, it passes and fails if the divergence regresses.

references.title: Issue #1696: project:render emits items that fail vbrief:validate on mixed-status scopes

expected_outputs: render -> validate passes by construction on the decompose fixture
shared status-derivation function backing render + D3 validate
swarm.missing_traces_justification: Traced to GitHub issue #1696 (repro + root cause) under umbrella #1699, not a spec section.
